programmes.show_description: The paramedics are called out to a man who's collapsed in the street and isn't breathing, a four-year-old who has a high temperature and a man with an abnormal heart rhythm.
At Nottingham's Queen's Medical Centre, seven-year-old Hunter needs help after falling from a tree. And 45-year-old Kirsty has had an accident at a fun run.
A woman is rushed in after a serious car crash. Simon fell off his bike on his way to buy a helmet. James, who severed his finger while climbing, talks about his love for his dad.
Kate's in Kenya, wondering: how do cold-brew and iced-coffee differ? Plus news from Staffordshire on why parsnips are so sweet. And do frozen pastries taste as nice as fresh ones?
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
Lee Juggurnauth helps Liz and Ian find their dream holiday home on Greece's Mani Peninsula, where they can enjoy the sunny climate and their love of outdoor sports, for a budget of £280k.
Laura Hamilton helps Dawn and her son Charlie look for the perfect holiday home for all their family and friends in the town of Tavira, on Portugal's eastern Algarve, for their £180k.
Mandy and Rae are planning a permanent move to Famagusta in eastern Cyprus. Jasmine Harman helps them find the perfect retirement property for their £150k budget.
Tricia and her daughter Jess welcome their rivals to St Hilary Guest House in Llandudno. There are plumbing problems and surprise revelations, and the hosts' cooking methods are questioned.
At The Falcon Retreat in Godreaman, some of the guests are wowed by the views, but breakfast doesn't sit well with everyone, and one couple's feedback has the hosts sharpening their talons.
Debbie and Paul welcome their rivals to The Crown Inn in Grewelthorpe, North Yorkshire. There's a creepy-crawly concern, and some sleep scores leave the hosts tossing and turning.
Darren and Alex host at their In the Stix glamping site in Greetham, Rutland. But one guest refuses to give it a go before even setting foot on site. Will anyone else up sticks?
It's the final day of the competition and time for the B&Bers to settle some scores. After dramatic discoveries and cutting comments, the payments are revealed and the winner is announced.
Oxford. The competition kicks off with Lyn, who serves an intriguingly named soup. Will her guests' competitive spirit knock Lyn's confidence during her summer solstice shenanigans? (Ep1/5)
Oxford. It's PhD student Kin's turn to host and he's hoping an Asian-inspired menu will bag him the cash. But when Kin forgets his marinade, everyone is left with dry meat. (Ep2/5)
Oxford. Kitchen designer Jess asks her guests to come dressed as their favourite idols, while she'll be Britney Spears! Sadly for Jess, her soggy souffles could prove toxic... (Ep3/5)
Oxford. Fundraiser Monica takes her guests on a culinary world tour, but there's not enough spice for Kin, and there's tension in the air. Can Monica keep her night under control? (Ep4/5)
Oxford. On the final night, host - and new boy - Ben has to go on the charm offensive after upsetting Lyn. But there's kitchen chaos and overcooked tuna. Can Ben save his night? (Ep5/5)
Artists Andrew and Diane host the week's first visit at their eclectic boutique accommodation in Devon, where one guest finds the facilities lacking.
In Portsmouth, Mubi and Steve focus on fun and wellness at their luxury lodgings. Unfortunately, a maintenance mishap gets them off to a bad start.
Alicia hosts the third visit of the week at her B&B in West Sussex, where the relaxed mood is pierced at evening drinks by an argument over online reviews.
The week's final visit is to Marc and Matt's gastropub in Kent. The pair have been building expectations throughout the week - will their place live up to the hype?
Will one guest's pernickety protests about a path prove pivotal in providing a plaque-winner on payment day? It's all kicking off!
This episode explores the importance of family. Mike, who's 79, has collapsed in the street. Chartered surveyor Andrew has tripped on a paving slab and has a suspected dislocated shoulder.
King's College Hospital in London has one of Britain's busiest A&E departments. In this episode, two cyclists arrive in A&E, and a retired docker takes a shine to nurse Laura.
This episode focuses on some of the youngest patients treated by King's College Hospital A&E, and meets 27-year-old emergency medicine trainee Matt.
Paramedics treat a man who's crashed his motorbike, a boy with chest pains and a family history of heart problems, a woman with abdominal pain and a baby who has a worrying cough.
Paramedics treat a young man who's collapsed outside a nightclub and isn't breathing, a young woman who's having a sickle cell crisis and a woman with crippling chest pains.
King's College Hospital in London has one of Britain's busiest A&E departments. In this episode, two cyclists arrive in A&E, and a retired docker takes a shine to nurse Laura.
This episode focuses on some of the youngest patients treated by King's College Hospital A&E, and meets 27-year-old emergency medicine trainee Matt.
More favourite investigations. Kate and Helen get the scoop on vanilla. In the Netherlands, Matt explores the myth that Edam balls were once used as emergency cannon balls!
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
Stroud. The first host, horse trainer Melanie, opts for a quick and easy, Italian-inspired vegetarian menu so she can focus on a glamorous night of hosting... and partying! (Ep1/5)
Stroud. Second host Elliot has made a good impression on one of his guests, but others are annoyed by simmering dinner table tensions and boastful claims - about livestock. (Ep2/5)
Stroud. Belarusian Tatsy serves up a taste of her culture and heritage complete with Russian dancing and pretend figure skating. But will guest grumbles leave her night on thin ice? (Ep3/5)
Stroud. Marketing executive Liv serves up a menu inspired by Italy, despite having never been there! Will her arancini and homemade ravioli make up for her rather lumpy panna cotta? (Ep4/5)
Stroud. There's rice rivalry on the final night, as radio presenter Kate serves up a risotto similar to Mel's. But can Kate's magical table decoration bag her the grand? (Ep5/5)
Andi and Neil dream of retiring to Spain with their son Joel and spaniel Dixie. Can Laura Hamilton find their perfect place close to the coastal town of Mazarrón, near Murcia, for £145,000?
Dawn and Ian want to fulfil their dream of owning a house in Tavira in Portugal. For £250k, will any Algarve offerings impress them enough to make an offer?
Harriet wants a holiday home in the popular resort of Villamartin on Spain's Costa Blanca. Property expert Lee Juggurnauth finds five fabulous homes for her budget of £110,000.
Uri and his wife Hazel are keen to find a permanent home in south west Cyprus, to escape the cold Scottish winters. Can Leah Charles-King find the perfect place for their £200k?
Portugal's Algarve is a very special place for Audrey and Tony, and they would love to buy their very own holiday home there. Lee Juggurnauth shows them what their £220k can buy.

programmes.show_name: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or...
programmes.show_description: ...List It: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer meet the Farhall family in Windsor. Nardeth is fed up with the layout of their four-bed detached home, but Graham loves the location.
Kirstie house-hunts with a pair of nervous first-time buyers in Balham and Putney in south west London. Phil looks for a big, Aussie-style property in the Thames Valley.
Na'im and Sara host first at their hotel in the breathtaking Cumbrian countryside. But one pair are too preoccupied with the furniture to care about the view.
Neil and Jack host at their hotel near majestic Windermere, where the B&Bers enjoy yet more stunning views. But there's still negativity in the feedback.
At Chris's glampsite in Richmond, North Yorkshire, off-grid really does mean off the electricity grid. Which is quite a worry for some of the B&Bers...
For the final visit the B&Bers go glamping again, at Phil and Jane's site in Derbyshire, where the stag-gering views include real-life deer wandering about the place.
On payment day, the B&Bers clash over furniture failings and clock complaints. And sparks fly - ironically - over a lack of electricity. Who's won?
Brand new series: Paramedics treat a man having a heart attack who thinks he's just pulled a muscle. And a day out hiking ends badly for a man who's fallen and injured his ankle.
This episode meets people who've taken big risks and explores how it's affected their lives. Motorbikers James and Peter are both rushed in. Ex-miner Alfred has severe chest pain.
Keith is rushed to Queen's after a cardiac arrest. Joanna has a spinal fracture after falling off a horse. And 79-year-old John has been run over by a car while out shopping.
